category: constitutional-gap (§6.V Container Emulators Mandate vs darwin/arm64 reality)
discovered_during: operator's directive 'Boot up Container(s) with proper Emulators using our Containers Submodule and continue all work now!' (post-session-12-commits anti-bluff sweep, HEAD b2828fb4)
summary: The §6.V mandate (added 2026-05-08) requires every Android emulator instance for Challenge Tests / UI verification to run inside a container managed by the vasic-digital/Containers submodule, rootless Podman/Docker only. The Lava-side implementation lives in docker/emulator/Dockerfile + docker-compose.test.yml. Confirmed structurally non-functional on darwin/arm64:

dockerfile_assumptions_that_fail: FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jdk → pulls arm64 image OK (Ubuntu 25.10 'resolute').
apt-get install qemu-kvm → arm64 Ubuntu marks qemu-kvm as a 'virtual package' with no installable provider (qemu-system-arm-hwe exists but is not a drop-in replacement for kvm acceleration).
apt-get install libgl1-mesa-glx → not available on Ubuntu 25.10 at all (obsoleted / removed in newer Ubuntu versions).
devices: /dev/kvm:/dev/kvm → /dev/kvm does not exist on the macOS host; the podman VM does not expose nested virt via HVF either.
evidence_commands: ls /dev/kvm → No such file or directory
podman info → Os: darwin, OsArch: darwin/arm64, os: linux (guest)
podman compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up -d lava-emulator → E: Package 'qemu-kvm' has no installation candidate; E: Package 'libgl1-mesa-glx' is not available; exit status 100

constitutional_classification: §6.V-debt — the Container Emulators Mandate was authored without a darwin/arm64 accommodation. The Decoupled Reusable Architecture rule says generic capability lives in Containers; the actual container runtime for that capability assumes Linux x86_64 (where KVM is available). On a darwin/arm64 workstation, the constitutional gate (§6.V) is unreachable today.

remediation_options.A_amend_§6.V: Add an explicit darwin/arm64 carve-out: 'on macOS-host development workstations, the host-direct emulator path via Containers/cmd/emulator-matrix is acceptable for Challenge Test iteration, but the gate (release-tag) MUST run on a Linux-x86_64 self-hosted runner where the container path works.' This is the lightest-touch remediation; preserves the gate's container-bound posture while unblocking workstation iteration.
remediation_options.B_harden_Dockerfile_for_arm64: Rewrite docker/emulator/Dockerfile to (a) use qemu-system-arm + Android arm64 system images instead of qemu-kvm, (b) drop libgl1-mesa-glx (Ubuntu 25.10 dropped it), (c) figure out HVF-or-something inside the podman VM on Apple Silicon. Apple Silicon nested virt is experimental at best; this path may not be solvable today. Estimated effort: 4-8 hours with uncertain payoff.
remediation_options.C_provision_linux_runner: Add a Linux-x86_64 self-hosted runner (operator's home server, cloud VM, or similar) where /dev/kvm is real and the existing Dockerfile works. The release-tag gate (per §6.I matrix) lives there; workstation iteration remains darwin/arm64-host-direct via Option A's carve-out.

recommended_action: A + C. Amend §6.V to carve out darwin/arm64-workstation host-direct; provision a Linux-x86_64 runner for the gate. Option B (Dockerfile-arm64) is a long-tail nice-to-have but not on the critical path.
actions_taken_in_this_session: Documented the gap (this file) — forensic record for the next agent to read.
Confirmed host-direct path works: ./submodules/containers/cmd/emulator-matrix builds clean from the pinned submodule, the binary has the documented Boot/Install/RunInstrumentation surface area.

Confirmed APK-build blocker is SEPARATE from the emulator-container gap: ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug fails on missing .env + keystores/debug.keystore + app/google-services.json regardless of which emulator path is used.
what_remains_unblocked_post_this_record: Emulator boot via Containers/cmd/emulator-matrix (host-direct on darwin/arm64) — feasible.
Multi-AVD matrix run for an already-built APK — feasible once an APK exists.
The §6.I matrix-attestation gate is reachable in spirit (Containers/cmd/emulator-matrix produces real-device-verification.json rows per AVD); the §6.V container-wrapper requirement is debt.
what_remains_blocked: APK build: requires operator-supplied .env, keystores/debug.keystore, app/google-services.json. None are present in the working tree (all gitignored).
Challenge Tests C04/C01/C27-C34 cannot run without an APK to install.
The §6.V CONTAINER-WRAPPER requirement on darwin/arm64 remains debt until Option A (amend) or B (harden) or C (Linux runner) ships.
